message:
security: mitigate CSV formula injection and log permission errors

Add formula injection protection to CSV escape_field(): values starting
with =, +, @, \t, \r, or a dash followed by a digit are now prefixed
with a single quote inside double quotes so spreadsheets treat them as
literal strings rather than formulas. Includes tests covering each
dangerous prefix and the benign-dash edge case.

Replace silent `let _ =` permission-setting calls in JSONL export paths
(export_to_jsonl_with_policy and write_jsonl_lines_atomically) with
tracing::warn! logging so failures to restrict file permissions are
visible in diagnostics instead of silently swallowed.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
